metadata.title: How to Use Claude
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 the one accent. Do not retint.
metadata.note: Fresh explainer — not from article source. Central claim: Claude output quality scales with context, not prompt templates. COLD OPEN, ILLUSTRATE, SPARK-LINE, HANDOFF, OUTRO laws in force.

beats.narration_text: This is Liam — in for Bear. Most people use Claude like a search engine. One question, one answer, close the tab. That's the wrong mental model — and it's why the output never quite lands. One idea to fix it. Let's see if it holds.

props.command: how do you actually get good output from Claude?
output: context is the product — not the prompt
the three things Claude nails every time

beats.narration_text: The common failure: you paste a question, get an answer, close the tab. Claude doesn't know your project. Doesn't know your constraints. Doesn't know what you already tried. It's writing a first draft of a response to a stranger. Output quality is bounded by the context you gave it — which was zero.

shot.needs: PIPELINE → fill media/B01.mp4 — diagram: one-shot session (no context) vs informed session

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The common failure: you paste a question, get an answer, close the tab. Claude doesn't know your project. Doesn't know your constraints. Doesn't know what you a

beats.narration_text: The mechanism is simple. Claude's quality scales with context. Not the model size. Not the prompt template. Context. The more Claude knows about your project, your constraints, and what good looks like for you — the closer its first draft is to your last draft. Every shortcut you take on context, you pay back in revision rounds.

shot.needs: PIPELINE → fill media/B02.mp4 — scale diagram: context input on x-axis, output quality on y-axis

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The mechanism is simple. Claude's quality scales with context. Not the model size. Not the prompt template. Context. The more Claude knows about your project, y

beats.narration_text: Projects solve this. Open Claude, create a Project for whatever you're building — a paper, a codebase, a campaign. Drop in a system prompt: your style guide, your constraints, what good output looks like. Every conversation in that Project inherits the context. Claude starts informed, not from scratch. One setup session. Every session after that: it already knows.

shot.needs: PIPELINE → fill media/B03.mp4 — diagram or screenshot of Projects UI showing system prompt + conversation history

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Projects solve this. Open Claude, create a Project for whatever you're building — a paper, a codebase, a campaign. Drop in a system prompt: your style guide, yo

beats.narration_text: Three things Claude is reliably good at. Artifacts — it produces rendered documents, code, and structured output you can actually use, not just text to copy. Analysis — hand it a paper, a dataset, or a codebase and it maps the structure before you tell it what to change. Rewriting — give it your rough draft and the register you're targeting; it hits the voice on the first pass. Where it fails: anything past its training cutoff, and anything requiring taste without examples of your taste.

shot.needs: PIPELINE → fill media/B04.mp4 — three-column card: Artifacts / Analysis / Rewriting with one-line each

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Three things Claude is reliably good at. Artifacts — it produces rendered documents, code, and structured output you can actually use, not just text to copy. An

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this into Claude — I'm starting [describe your project in one sentence]. My main constraint is [constraint]. What's the first question you need answered before you can actually help me? That prompt inverts the default. Instead of Claude guessing, it tells you exactly what context it needs. The question it asks back is the real diagnostic. Run it.

props.command: I'm starting [describe your project]. My main constraint is [constraint]. What's the first question you need answered before you can actually help me?

beats.narration_text: How to Use Claude. Subscribe if this saved you a read. Liam, in for Bear.
